qa_pairs.question: On a Jupyter Lab Desktop, what steps should a user take to create a custom desktop icon that starts Ansys Workbench in mesa mode when graphics appear corrupted?
qa_pairs.answer: If the graphics of an application started within Ansys Workbench appear unusable due to corruption on a Jupyter Lab Desktop, first copy the existing `workbench.desktop` file to `~/Desktop/workbench-mesa.desktop`. Next, open the newly created file in a text editor (e.g., nano) and change all instances of `runwb2` to `runwb2 -oglmesa`. Finally, refresh the Jupyter desktop by pressing `Control-R` to make the new icon appear, which will persist for future sessions until manually deleted.
